name: Stuffed Acorn Squash
recipeIngredient: 6 oz Lean ground Pork or Turkey
recipeInstructions:
1. Spray a 10x6x2 baking dish with Pam.
2. Halve the squash and discard seeds. Place cut sides down in the baking dish.
3. Bake uncovered at 350°F for 50 minutes.
4. Meanwhile, prepare stuffing: Cook ground pork or turkey with celery and onion until meat is no longer pink and vegetables are tender. Drain excess fat.
5. Stir in salt, curry powder, and cinnamon; cook for an additional minute.
6. Add applesauce and bread cubes. Mix well.
7. Turn squash cut sides up in the dish. Place stuffing into squash halves.
8. Bake uncovered for 20 more minutes.
MICROWAVE DIRECTIONS:
1. Place squash, cut side down, in a 10x6x2-inch microwave-safe baking dish.
2. Cook on high power (100%) for 6½-8½ minutes or until tender.
3. Set aside.
4. In a 1-quart casserole, cook ground pork or turkey with celery and onion, covered, on high for 2-3 minutes or until meat is no longer pink, stirring twice.
5. Drain excess fat from the meat.
6. Stir in curry powder, salt, and cinnamon; cook for an additional 30 seconds.
7. Add applesauce and bread cubes. Mix well.
8. Spoon into squash halves.
9. Cook uncovered on high for 3-4 minutes or until heated through.
